claim: K-shaped future of software: systems thinking, judgment, and problem framing are accelerating, while low-level language syntax and formatting become less critical
actionable: Shift career development focus from syntax mastery to high-level systems thinking, architectural judgment, and problem decomposition
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Anush Elangovan: Impact of AI on Software

claim: Intent velocity—how fast ideas move from conception to production—is the new primary measure of success, replacing lines of code
actionable: Measure team productivity and AI impact by time-to-production for ideas, not by code volume or hours spent coding
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Anush Elangovan: Impact of AI on Software

claim: Agents should run autonomously in parallel at night or during non-work hours, multiplying effective throughput; this was not feasible before agentic AI
actionable: Structure agent workflows to run unsupervised in parallel overnight, setting intent and constraints upfront rather than manual sequential execution
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Anush Elangovan: Impact of AI on Software

claim: Agents now monitor bugs, file pull requests, and validate code autonomously in production workflows; this automation was literally not feasible six months prior
actionable: Implement autonomous bug monitoring agents that identify issues, create PRs, and run CI/CD validation without human intervention
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Anush Elangovan: Impact of AI on Software

claim: AI projects that seemed impossible six months ago are now overnight projects; teams are 100x more productive when AI is applied to entire stack layers
actionable: Re-evaluate technical roadmaps; tasks previously deemed infeasible (like hardware-GPU tensor translation in 48 hours) may now be viable with agentic AI
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Anush Elangovan: Impact of AI on Software
